discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with risk-on rotation into financials, healthcare, defense, and small-caps (KRE +3%, XLV +3%, ITA +3%, IWM +1.5%) while semis got hit hard (SMH -1.6%) on AVGO's ~12.6% plunge. Gene-editing names ripping in sympathy as a group, while FIVE collapses on a fresh earnings 8-K. VIX down to 15.4 confirms the broader risk-on backdrop outside of tech.

notable_index_moves: SMH -1.63% driven by AVGO -12.6% — semiconductor weakness against an otherwise risk-on tape
XLF +2.59% and KRE +3.09% — strong regional bank/financials bid
ITA +2.97% — defense complex strong despite Trump reluctance-to-resume-Iran-war headlines (counterintuitive)
VIX -4% to 15.4 — overall risk appetite intact

discoveries.name: Five Below, Inc.
discoveries.thesis: A sharp double-digit decline on the day of an earnings 8-K, in the context of LULU also cutting guidance and citing undisclosed headwinds, fits a justified earnings disappointment in discretionary retail. The five-day move closely matches today's move, suggesting the drop is the earnings reaction itself rather than an overshoot. Magnitude looks appropriate for a guide-down at a stock that had been priced for continued execution.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibits show comp-store sales miss and/or FY guide cut; management call cites tariff/consumer headwinds similar to LULU's language; sell-side downgrades follow tomorrow.
discoveries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a beat with conservative guide that was misread; or a one-time charge (inventory write-down) that's non-recurring, in which case the move would be OVERDONE rather than RATIONAL.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a discretionary retailer prints earnings the same day a major peer (LULU) issues a guide-cut, treat sympathy-plus-own-print as a compounding event — the move is rarely a fade on day one.
discoveries.catalyst: Q1 earnings release filed via 8-K on 2026-06-03 (item 2.02)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K item 2.02 dated 2026-06-03 confirms earnings release; LULU same-day guide cut establishes sector context.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit (press release) for FY26 guidance vs. consensus
Compare comp-store sales trend to prior quarter; check for tariff exposure callout
Cross-check inventory levels and gross margin commentary against LULU's 'undisclosed headwinds' language
Look at options skew/put pricing for tomorrow — gauge whether a bounce is being faded

discoveries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing peer group rally with company-specific 8-K (officer change + charter amendment)
discoveries.thesis: CRSP is up high single digits alongside NTLA (+13%) and BEAM (+11%) — the cohort is moving together, which suggests a sector/platform catalyst (possibly clinical data, regulatory tailwind, or M&A speculation). CRSP additionally has an 8-K today with items 5.02 (officer change), 5.03 (charter amendment), and 5.07 (typically annual meeting vote results) — the 5.07 combined with 5.03 strongly suggests routine annual meeting governance, not a destabilizing event. If the group catalyst is real and CRSP has the deepest pipeline + Casgevy commercial ramp, it should outperform peers on follow-through rather than lag them.
discoveries.what_confirms: Identify a specific sector catalyst (FDA action, conference data, large-pharma M&A in gene editing); confirm the 5.02 is a routine board addition rather than a CEO/CFO departure; Casgevy revenue commentary turns positive.
discoveries.what_kills: The 5.02 turns out to be a key executive (CEO/CSO/CFO) departing under non-routine circumstances — then the move is sympathy-only and CRSP should lag peers, not lead. Also kills the thesis if NTLA/BEAM rally fades by tomorrow.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Annual meeting 8-Ks (5.03 + 5.07 together) are usually governance boilerplate and should not be confused with material 5.02 departures — read the exhibit before assuming severity.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector-wide gene-editing rally + same-day 8-K with governance items (likely annual meeting)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K combines 5.02/5.03/5.07/9.01 — the 5.07 + 5.03 pattern is the classic annual meeting filing; combined with NTLA/BEAM moving together points to a sector catalyst, not company-specific bad news.
research_pointers: Identify what's moving NTLA/BEAM/CRSP together today — check ASH/ASCO/conference calendar, FDA dockets, gene-therapy M&A chatter
Read CRSP's 8-K exhibits — confirm 5.02 is a routine board election vs. an unexpected departure
Check options flow for any specific name in the cohort — leader/laggard tells you who has the catalyst
Look for Casgevy commercial update or partner Vertex (VRTX) commentary

discoveries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing cohort rally with no SEC filings or scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: NTLA leads the gene-editing group up double digits on elevated but not extreme volume, with empty catalyst_signals. Without an 8-K, a press release in news flow, or a conference data point I can point to, I cannot determine whether this is a clinical data leak, M&A speculation, or short-covering in a beaten-down name. Both a bull case (real catalyst, follow-through) and a bear case (squeeze fades) are plausible but neither articulates at conf 3+ without more evidence.
discoveries.what_confirms: A specific catalyst surfaces (data readout, partnership, takeover rumor) tying the move to NTLA specifically as group leader.
discoveries.what_kills: Move reverses tomorrow with no follow-through and no identified catalyst — confirms squeeze/noise.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; no news in the feed; group move suggests sector catalyst but source is not visible in available data.
research_pointers: Search for NTLA-specific news in the last 24 hours (Twitter/X, biotech newsletters, conference schedules)
Check short interest and borrow rates — large biotech squeezes have characteristic signatures
Cross-reference any ATTR amyloidosis or HAE program updates

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense complex rally despite Iran de-escalation headline
discoveries.thesis: ITA is up nearly 3% and Kratos, AVAV, MRCY are all up mid-to-high single digits. The counterintuitive part: Trump-reluctant-to-resume-Iran-war should pressure defense, yet the group is bid. More likely explanation is a budget/appropriations catalyst, a specific drone/unmanned program award, or rotation into industrials (XLI +1.2%). With volume actually below average on KTOS and AVAV, this looks like a coordinated rotation/multi-day positioning rather than a news spike. Magnitude appears appropriate to a sector rotation move.
discoveries.what_confirms: DoD budget headline, drone/UAS program award, or congressional appropriations news; if MRCY/AVAV/KTOS all continue moving together over coming days.
discoveries.what_kills: Stocks reverse with no identifiable catalyst — would suggest a one-day squeeze rather than rotation.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Below-average-volume rallies in a thematic group (defense, gene editing) often signal rotation/positioning rather than news — they tend to continue more than news-spike moves with high volume.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals across KTOS/AVAV/MRCY combined with ITA +3% on the day points to sector rotation, not name-specific news.
research_pointers: Check for FY27 DoD budget request leaks or House/Senate Armed Services Committee markup news
Review drone/CCA (Collaborative Combat Aircraft) program updates — KTOS Valkyrie
Look at AVAV's recent BlueHalo integration commentary

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of today's moving stocks (FIVE, NTLA, BEAM, CRSP, KTOS, AVAV, MRCY, VECO, ALGN) have meaningful linkage to today's AI announcements. FIVE -13.78% is an earnings reaction (8-K item 2.02 filed 6/3). Gene editing names (NTLA, BEAM, CRSP) moving together suggests a sector-specific biotech catalyst (possibly ASGCT/conference-driven), unrelated to AI news. Defense names (KTOS, AVAV, MRCY) moving together suggests a defense/geopolitical catalyst, not AI. VECO and ALGN moves lack clear AI-news linkage. No actionable AI-driven irrational selloffs or under-reactions identified in today's tape.
